name: codex-config-toml-protected
doc: Precondition of the neverApproval exemption: an agent running approval_policy=never must never be able to self-elevate by writing an allow rule into its OWN `.codex/config.toml` or `.codex/rules/` — independent of Codex's sandbox_mode, since even danger-full-access never covers the user's ~/.codex/ (verified 2026-07-10, Codex's own "Protected paths" guard only scopes a project-local .codex/ inside a workspace-write root). protected-path.ts's PROTECTED_FRAGMENTS now blocks both a direct Write and a Bash redirect into either path.

steps.doc: A direct Write to .codex/config.toml — blocked like any other protected fragment (protected-path.ts:87-89).
A Bash redirect into the same file — extractWriteTargets picks up the `>` target, blocked the same way (protected-path.ts:57-73).
A Write into .codex/rules/ — the second new fragment, an allow-rule directory that would be an equally viable self-elevation vector.
